included: Solution sets S(A,b), null spaces N(A), and spans as sets
Subset proofs by taking an arbitrary element
Set equality as two subset directions
Redundant-vector span equality as a model two-inclusion proof
excluded: Long induction proofs for many redundant vectors
All assignment 3 invertibility and nilpotence applications

beats.label: membership
beats.content: A set is treated as an object; x belongs to S, while a smaller collection can be a subset of S.

beats.label: set-builder notation
beats.content: The ambient space names the objects; the condition selects which objects are collected.

beats.label: solution sets
beats.content: S(A,b)={x in R^n: Ax=b}; empty, singleton, and parameterized solution sets are all set statements.

beats.label: recurring sets
beats.content: N(A) and Span{u_i} are sets described by equations or parameters, not merely lists.

beats.label: subset proof
beats.content: To prove S subset T, take arbitrary x in S, translate definitions, and show x satisfies T.

beats.label: set equality
beats.content: To prove S=T, prove S subset T and T subset S; redundant-vector span equality follows this routine.

exportFallback: Exports should include the localized static video-study frame sequence and no raw video paths.
widgetDecision: Manim-only. The useful teaching move is a fixed proof-language routine; a widget would duplicate existing span and solution-set controls without adding enough value.
